steps.title: Isolate m
steps.content:
Starting from $7m = 2(n+p)$, divide both sides by $7$:
$$m = \frac{2(n+p)}{7}$$

steps.title: Check each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A ($m = \frac{2(n+p)}{7}$): exactly the result of dividing by $7$.
  • Choice B ($m = 2(n+p)$): forgets to divide by $7$.
  • Choice C ($m = 2(n+p) - 7$): subtracts $7$ instead of dividing — wrong inverse operation.
  • Choice D ($m = 2 - n - p - 7$): distributes and subtracts incorrectly.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
